description: Regression hunt: Prometheus recording rule performance regression (3-5x slowdown due to head posting optimization)

required_findings.description: Identifies tsdb/querier.go or querier/Select as the changed component
Mentions head posting optimization or SelectSorted optimization
Explains that the optimization removed sorting when only one block (head block)
Identifies that optimization caused full head scan before filtering
Mentions the fix (PR #7051 or revert commit 635fff9)
Mentions issue #6968 or #7048 (reports of the regression)
Identifies performance regression magnitude (3-5x slowdown or similar)
Mentions high cardinality or large number of series as the trigger condition
Identifies that regression specifically affects head block queries (not TSDB blocks)
Mentions recording rule evaluation or rule group evaluation as the symptom context
Identifies CPU overhead or increased resource consumption

file_references.description: Identifies tsdb/querier.go (where Select and head posting logic lives)
Identifies tsdb/index/ or posting list related files
Identifies promql/ or rules/ directory (where recording rules are evaluated)
References benchmark files or performance tests

causal_chain.description: Hop 1: Commit 52630ad modified Select() in tsdb/querier.go to optimize the case where only one block (head block) exists, removing the sorting step
Hop 2: The optimization assumed sorted iteration wasn't needed when only querying the head block, so it skipped SelectSorted and used direct iteration
Hop 3: Without sorted iteration, the query had to scan the full head posting list before applying matchers/filters, instead of using index optimizations to pre-filter

Hop 4: For high-cardinality metrics with millions of series, scanning all series before filtering caused 3-5x increase in query execution time, manifesting as recording rule evaluation slowdown (200s → 3000s)

negative_checks.description: Does NOT blame TSDB compaction issues as the root cause
Does NOT claim it's a memory leak or memory management issue
Does NOT blame network, remote storage, or remote write as the cause
Does NOT claim it's a scrape configuration or scrape interval issue
